topic: data/wins.json on main is currently unparseable as JSON
change_summary: Encountered during the standard Step 0 fresh-clone read for change-driven review. node and python3 both fail to parse data/wins.json with 'Expecting comma delimiter: line 1411 column 2374 (char 418561)'. Reproduced on HEAD (3b40338e), the previous workspace-sync commit (d09d9780, 2026-05-08T02:05:18Z), and confirmed by tests/test.js which reports 'FAIL: wins.json parses as valid JSON'. Last-good commit is 6e68bba7 (workspace-sync 2026-05-07T14:05:16Z).

failure_signature.introducing_commit_message: Workspace sync: 2026-05-08T02:05:18Z — Auto-committed workspace-only files from FUSE mount.

root_cause: An archive-cite span containing inline HTML attributes was added to WIN-064 detail_extra without escaping the embedded double quotes. The added span fragment reads (verbatim from the diff): [both quotes archived verbatim at raw-text/02b-wins-V51-win064.txt, captured 2026-05-08; live page MD5 16d65a42722d2d5869842d14c8f699f0 has been byte-identical across 34 consecutive poller checks (~17 days through 2026-05-07T11:05:55Z), so the citation is auditable against both our archive and the dome's currently-served page]. The class= and href= attribute values use raw double quotes inside a JSON string value, where they must be backslash-escaped (\"archive-cite\", \"https://...\"). The same edit's surrounding text (e.g., a wins.html#win-064 link earlier in the same field) IS correctly escaped, so this is a localized escape miss, not a global encoding problem.
scope_of_impact: Build pipeline: build.js cannot regenerate docs/index.html from wins.json — site rebuilds will fail.
Test suite: tests/run.sh wins.json schema gate is failing (per test.js dry run).

All other agents that read data/wins.json via JSON.parse will throw on first read: decider, analyst (any mode that reads wins.json), integrity (next daily run), social, tinker. Only the curmudgeon (this run) and any agent that reads raw text directly will function.

Latest integrity summary (2026-05-08T01:16Z) reports 'TESTS: 3519 passed, 0 failed ✅' and 'BUILD: PASS' — but that integrity run preceded the bad commit (commit at 02:05Z, ~49 minutes after integrity ran). The next integrity run (daily 9 AM) should catch this; until then, no automated alarm is in place.

FUSE workspace mount data/wins.json (mtime 2026-05-08 01:19 UTC) carries the same broken content; the workspace-sync at 02:05Z propagated FUSE-side breakage to git, so the source of truth on FUSE is also broken.
containment: This curmudgeon run read WIN-057 content from the last-good commit 6e68bba7 to complete the regularly-scheduled change-driven review. WIN-057 content is byte-identical between 6e68bba7 and HEAD, so the review uses authentic current content even though current HEAD is unparseable. No content was modified by this curmudgeon run; the only writes are review files in monitor/curmudgeon/.
recommended_actions: 1. Decider (or operator manually): edit data/wins.json on the FUSE workspace, locate the WIN-064 detail_extra archive-cite span, and escape the four embedded raw double quotes (around 'archive-cite' class value, around the github URL href value, and the closing quotes for each). The cleanest mechanical fix is to replace the entire span fragment with a properly-JSON-encoded version generated by JSON.stringify on the intended HTML payload. After the fix, run tests/run.sh in the clean clone to confirm parse passes.

2. Add a pre-commit / pre-push gate to workspace-sync that JSON.parse-validates data/wins.json before pushing. Per CLAUDE.md 'Run Tests Before Committing Data Changes' rule, this is the rule that should have caught it. The workspace-sync agent currently does not run tests/run.sh. Suggest tinker file an issue or PROP for adding the parse-validation gate to workspace-sync.md.

3. Identify whether the bad edit originated from the decider, analyst, or operator. The diff is purely additive (the archive-cite citation was inserted into an existing detail_extra), so it likely came from a decider patch or an operator manual edit on FUSE. Audit recent decider patch records around 2026-05-08T01-19 (FUSE mtime) and 2026-05-08T02-05 (sync) to identify the responsible writer.

4. Consider widening the broken-state-detection: the daily 9 AM integrity run will catch this, but the gap between bad commit (02:05Z) and detection (~9:00Z) is 7 hours. If the workspace-sync ran a wins.json parse-check on each cycle (hourly), the detection lag would shrink to ~1h.
defensive_lens: A dome defender who clones the repo at this moment cannot run our build. They could file an issue on GitHub mocking the data integrity of a project that publishes scientific arguments. Severity 'critical' is the right tag both because it blocks the pipeline and because the public-facing optics are bad if the broken state persists into the next dome-side news cycle.
notes_for_decider: This is a non-WIN, non-section, non-prediction review. It is filed under monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/ as INFRA-WINS-JSON-PARSE-2026-05-08.json so the digest pipeline picks it up. Severity is 'critical' for alert-triage purposes. No queue_id (this finding was discovered during a Priority 3 change-driven scan, not a queue item).
